text.title: Belief-Update Agent Pipeline
text.summary: Daily/weekly agent pipeline for maintaining versioned beliefs.yaml, prompt patterns with XML structure, and critical gotchas.

section.title: Pipeline Architecture
section.p: signal-collector (haiku, daily) → events.ndjson. signal-classifier (haiku/sonnet, daily) → tags evidence-for | evidence-against | new-hypothesis | noise. belief-updater (sonnet, weekly) → versioned beliefs.yaml diff. weekly-synthesizer (sonnet, Monday) → digest with cited event_ids. strategy-reviewer (opus, Friday) → keep|kill|reframe decisions with owner and check-back date. decision-logger (haiku, continuous) → prediction + check-back date per decision.

rule.statement: Separate mechanical work (haiku: signal collection, classification, decision logging) from judgment work (opus: strategy review, roadmap pass). Mixing costs 10x and introduces bias.
rule.rationale: Opus applied to classification tasks burns budget without improving quality. Haiku applied to strategy synthesis produces confident-sounding but shallow reasoning. Each model tier has a specific function.

example.code: <role>belief-updater</role> <current_beliefs>{{ beliefs_yaml }}</current_beliefs> <new_events>{{ events_jsonl }}</new_events> <rules> Only mutate a belief if (a) >=3 independent event_ids agree, OR (b) 1 high-severity event directly contradicts current claim with cited URL/transcript. Never invent beliefs. Cite event_id for every change. </rules> <output_schema> {changed:[{belief_id, before, after, evidence:[event_id], confidence_delta}], unchanged_with_pressure:[{belief_id, opposing_evidence:[event_id]}]} </output_schema>

<role>strategy-reviewer</role> <beliefs>{{ beliefs_yaml }}</beliefs> <roadmap>{{ roadmap_md }}</roadmap> <experiments>{{ experiments_yaml }}</experiments> <deliverable>For each roadmap item: keep|kill|reframe, cited evidence, decision_owner, check_back_date.</deliverable> <constraint>Default to "kill" if evidence_for_keeping < 3 cited events in last 30 days. Never reuse last week's recommendation without re-citing.</constraint>

antipattern.description: Memory rot: belief-updater pulled into context-window compaction loses last week's reasoning.
antipattern.reason: Persist beliefs.yaml and beliefs.history.ndjson outside the LLM and re-inject on every run. Never rely on LLM memory for belief continuity across sessions.

antipattern.description: Evidence laundering: sonnet rewrites the same event into 3 different "independent" supports for a belief.
antipattern.reason: Dedupe by event_id hash before counting independent signals. Three occurrences of the same event do not count as three independent signals.

antipattern.description: Hallucinated event_ids: model invents event_42 that does not exist in events.ndjson.
antipattern.reason: Run a fact-checker pass that looks up every cited id in events.ndjson before publishing. Uncited event_ids are fabrications.

antipattern.description: Token blow-up from dumping 10k events directly into Opus weekly.
antipattern.reason: Pre-cluster with haiku, hand 50–100 archetypal events to opus. Full event streams at opus cost is wasteful and counterproductively degrades reasoning quality.

antipattern.description: Action-less insight loop: agent surfaces 200 insights per week, no decision_owner, nobody acts.
antipattern.reason: Force schema field decision_owner on every event that reaches the belief-updater. Drop events without an owner rather than surfacing them without action.

text.reference: Weekly belief-update loop: loads events.ndjson, calls Claude, writes beliefs.yaml
